title: Extracting nucleic acids from viruses on a filter
author: Grieg F. Steward and Alexander I. Culley
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.dsm6c5

steps.title: Prepare injection syringe with lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL T + C lysis buffer containing 100 µg/ml proteinase K to a low-volume (1–3 cc) syringe that has been fitted with a female–female Luer-Lok adapter (the injection syringe). It is convenient to use a larger syringe (10–20 cc) as an extraction buffer reservoir. The injection syringe can be easily filled with the proper volume by connecting it tip to tip with the reservoir syringe via the adapter. The reservoir can be used to fill multiple injection syringes if more than one sample is to be extracted.

steps.title: Connect injection syringe to filter
steps.instruction: Ensure that there is minimal air in the injection syringe-adapter assembly, then connect it to the outlet of the filter.

steps.title: Connect aspiration syringe to filter inlet
steps.instruction: Connect a second low-volume syringe to the filter inlet (the aspiration syringe).

steps.title: Position filter-syringe assembly vertically
steps.instruction: Hold the filter-syringe assembly vertically with the injector syringe pushing upward from below.

steps.title: Push extraction buffer into filter
steps.instruction: Hold the filter securely to the injection syringe and gently, but firmly, push extraction buffer into the filter housing until liquid just begins to appear in the aspiration syringe.

steps.title: Incubate filter assembly at 65°C
steps.instruction: Incubate the assembly (filter with two syringes attached) for 15 min at 65°C in a hybridization oven. It is helpful to connect the syringe-filter assembly to a rotisserie so that the entire filter surface is wetted in the event that bubbles are present in the housing. The syringes on either side of the filter can be secured to the clips of the rotisserie with elastic bands.

steps.title: Cool syringe-filter assembly
steps.instruction: Allow the syringe-filter assembly to cool briefly.

steps.title: Remove extract to aspiration syringe
steps.instruction: Remove the extract by holding the syringe assembly vertically with the aspiration syringe underneath (and the filter upside down) and gently pulling on the plunger to pull the extract into the aspiration syringe.

steps.title: Detach aspiration syringe
steps.instruction: Detach the aspiration syringe.

steps.title: Transfer extract to microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the extract to a microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Chill on ice
steps.instruction: Chill on ice for 2–3 minutes.

steps.title: Add protein precipitation reagent and vortex
steps.instruction: Add one-half volume of MPC protein precipitation reagent (supplied in the kit) and vortex for 10 s.

steps.title: Pellet debris by centrifugation
steps.instruction: Pellet the debris by centrifugation at 10,000g for 10 min.

steps.title: Transfer supernatant carefully
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant (containing the nucleic acids) to a sterile microcentrifuge tube; be very careful to avoid the pellet (containing the SDS-protein complex). All or some of the sample may be archived at this point by freezing at –80°C.

steps.title: Transfer sample and add polyacryl carrier
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 800 µl of the sample to a fresh tube; add 1 µl polyacryl carrier, and vortex briefly.

steps.title: Add polyacryl carrier (optional)
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl polyacryl carrier, and vortex briefly. Carrier is optional, but can improve yields when working with nanogram to subnanogram quantities of nucleic acid.

steps.title: Add isopropanol
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of 100% isopropanol.

steps.title: Mix by inverting
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting the tube several times.

steps.title: Centrifuge sample to pellet nucleic acids
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at ≥10,000g for 15–45 min. Longer centrifugation times can improve the yields for small amounts of nucleic acids, especially in the absence of carrier.

steps.title: Decant or aspirate supernatant cautiously
steps.instruction: Decant or aspirate the supernatant. Use caution, the pellet can dislodge easily and be lost.
